mission.title: Install and Configure Redis Operator (Spotahome / OT-CONTAINER-KIT)
mission.description: The Redis Operator by OT-CONTAINER-KIT simplifies the deployment and management of Redis instances on Kubernetes, supporting both standalone and clustered setups. It provides built-in monitoring and adheres to best practices for security and performance. This operator is ideal for teams looking to streamline their Redis deployments in a Kubernetes environment.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Redis Operator Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add redis-operator https://opstree.github.io/redis-operator

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Redis Operator repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Redis Operator
steps.description:
This command installs the Redis Operator in the 'redis-operator' namespace, creating the namespace if it doesn't exist.

```bash

kubectl create namespace redis-operator

helm install redis-operator redis-operator/redis-operator --namespace redis-operator --version 0.24.0

```

> Creates the namespace for the Redis Operator.
> Installs the Redis Operator with the specified version.

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the Redis Operator pods are running correctly in the specified namespace.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n redis-operator

```

> Lists all pods in the redis-operator namespace to verify their status.

steps.title: Create a Redis Cluster Custom Resource
steps.description:
This command creates a sample Redis Cluster Custom Resource to deploy a Redis cluster managed by the operator.

```bash

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: redis.opstree.dev/v1
kind: Redis
metadata:
  name: my-redis
  namespace: redis-operator
spec:
  mode: cluster
  replicas: 3
EOF

```

> Creates a Redis cluster with 3 replicas.
commands:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: redis.opstree.dev/v1
kind: Redis
metadata:
  name: my-redis
  namespace: redis-operator
spec:
  mode: cluster
  replicas: 3
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Redis Cluster
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Redis cluster to ensure it is running as expected.

```bash

kubectl get redis -n redis-operator

```

> Lists all Redis instances in the redis-operator namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Redis Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Redis Operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all Redis instances managed by the operator, leading to potential data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall redis-operator --namespace redis-operator

kubectl delete namespace redis-operator

```

> Uninstalls the Redis Operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Redis Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your Redis data. This step will upgrade the Redis Operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade redis-operator redis-operator/redis-operator --namespace redis-operator --version 0.24.0

kubectl get pods -n redis-operator

```

> Drains the node to prepare for upgrade.
> Upgrades the Redis Operator to the specified version.
> Verifies that the pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create Redis instance'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Redis Operator may not have sufficient permissions to create resources.

**Fix:**
Ensure the operator has the correct RBAC permissions configured.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Redis configuration may be invalid or the image may not be compatible.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the Redis pods for errors and validate the configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Redis cluster not forming
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Network policies or firewall rules may be blocking communication between Redis pods.

**Fix:**
Review and adjust network policies to allow traffic between Redis pods.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Redis instance not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified Redis instance may not exist or was deleted.

**Fix:**
Verify the Redis instance name and check if it exists in the cluster.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Redis 7.x, improved monitoring features, and enhanced TLS configuration options.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v0.19 or earlier, review the new configuration options for Redis 7.x.

resolution.summary: You should see the Redis instance listed with 'Running' status when executing `kubectl get redis -n redis-operator`.
codeSnippets:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: redis.opstree.dev/v1
kind: Redis
metadata:
  name: my-redis
  namespace: redis-operator
spec:
  mode: cluster
  replicas: 3
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.
